description: A graceful, tall annual with feathery foliage and large, daisy-like flowers in shades of pink, white, and crimson. Loved by pollinators and perfect for cutting.

characteristics.waterNeeds: high and THEN info: Moderate — drought tolerant once established
characteristics.soilType: Poor to moderately fertile, well-drained soil

care.sowIndoors: Sow thinly in trays or modules of seed compost. Cover lightly with vermiculite or compost. Keep at 15–25°C. Germination takes 7–14 days.
care.sowOutdoors: Sow directly into prepared soil once the risk of frost has passed (late May). Thin seedlings to 30–45cm apart.
care.hardenOff: Acclimatise young plants to outdoor conditions for 7–10 days before planting out after the last frost.
care.plantOut: Plant into their final positions in full sun. Space 30–45cm apart. Stake tall varieties if in a windy spot.
care.watering: Water regularly until established. Once growing well, they are fairly drought tolerant but appreciate water during dry spells.
care.feeding: Generally not required. Too much fertiliser produces lush foliage at the expense of flowers.
care.harvest: Cut flowers regularly for indoor vases. Pick when the petals are just opening.
care.overwintering: Annual — discard plants on the compost heap after the first hard frost.

propagation.seedDescription: Long, thin, dark brown seeds resembling small sticks.

deadheading.notes: Deadhead spent blooms regularly to encourage continuous flowering until the first frosts.

seedSaving.method: Allow some flower heads to turn brown and dry on the plant. Collect the seeds on a dry day and store in a cool, dry place.
seedSaving.seedPodDescription: Seeds form in a cluster at the center of the old flower head.

commonProblems: Slugs and snails — especially on young seedlings.
Powdery mildew in late summer if crowded or dry at the roots.
funFact: The name Cosmos comes from the Greek word for 'order' or 'harmony', referring to the plant's evenly placed petals.
